details: The "Academic Education" scheme by the Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, Labour Department, Jammu & Kashmir, provides financial assistance to the children of the registered workers. Under this scheme, regular students in Class 9th to 10th receive ₹4,500/-, Class 11th to 12th receive ₹6,000/-, undergraduate students receive ₹10,000/-, and postgraduate students receive ₹15,000/-.
benefits: Financial Assistance based on the Educational Qualification -
eligibility: For Registration as a Building/ Construction Worker -
The applicant should be a resident of Jammu & Kashmir.
The applicant should be a Building/ Construction Worker.
The applicant should be between 18 and 60 years.
The applicant should have engaged in any building or other construction work for at least 90 days during the preceding 12 months.
The registration of the applicant should be renewed annually.
The parent/s must be registered member/s of the "Jammu & Kashmir Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board".
The parent/s must have an active membership with the JKBOCWW Board.
The child must be a student.
The child must be a regular in attendance.

offline: Go to the J&K Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (JKBOCWWB)
On the homepage, find and click the "
If you're a new user, click on "Apply for New Registration."
Complete the registration form with your details, submit the form, and send the OTP to your registered mobile number. Enter the OTP and click "Verify OTP & Submit."
After the OTP is verified, a confirmation message with your Login ID and password will be sent to your registered mobile number.
Use the Login ID, password, and captcha to log in (remember, the username and password are case-sensitive). filled are case-sensitive). Read all the instructions carefully and Click to continue
Once logged in, choose the "Construction Worker’s Registration" option and click on "Registration Form."
A dashboard will appear. Complete the form by entering all required details (marked with *).
Upload all the important documents, such as ID proof and other relevant certificates.
Once the form is completed and documents uploaded, click "Submit" and pay the application fee online. You will receive a registration number after successful payment and submission.

documents_required: Proof of Residence (e.g., Utility Bill, Voter ID, etc.)
Bank Account Details (e.g., Copy of Bank Passbook)
Age Certificate (any one of the following):
Certificate issued by District Medical Board (if both of the above are unavailable)
Identity Card of the registered member (parent)

faqs.question: What is the Academic Education Scheme?
faqs.answer: The scheme provides financial assistance to the children of registered construction workers for their education from Class 9th to post-graduation.

faqs.question: Who is eligible for the Academic Education Scheme?
faqs.answer: Children of registered workers with the Jammu & Kashmir Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board are eligible.

faqs.question: Is the scheme available for students of all educational streams?
faqs.answer: Yes, the scheme applies to regular students across all streams.

faqs.question: How can I apply for the Academic Education Scheme?
faqs.answer: You can apply by logging in to the official website of the Welfare Board and selecting "Apply for Scheme."

faqs.question: What documents are required for applying?
faqs.answer: Documents such as the member's Identity Card, student ID card, student photograph, marksheet, and birth certificate are required.

faqs.question: Does the worker need to be registered with the Welfare Board?
faqs.answer: Yes, the worker must be a registered member of the Jammu & Kashmir Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.

faqs.question: Are there any specific work requirements for the worker?
faqs.answer: Yes, the worker must have completed at least 90 days of building or construction work in the last 12 months.

faqs.question: How is the financial assistance provided?
faqs.answer: The amount is typically transferred directly to the worker's bank account.

faqs.question: Is this scheme available to workers from outside Jammu & Kashmir?
faqs.answer: No, the scheme is only available to workers registered in Jammu & Kashmir.

faqs.question: Can part-time students avail of this scheme?
faqs.answer: No, the scheme is only for regular students.
